title: Mistranslation in volume 11
wikitext:
[]: Mistranslation in volume 11?             Quote Post by COACH » Tue Sep 07, 2010 2:06 am So I finally got around to buying VIZ's translation of volume 11 of Sadamoto's manga, and I noticed an odd line: Kaworu, page 60 wrote: If I did not have a soul, we could be come one.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Sep 07, 2010 3:19 am Thank you for this thread, I didn't notice that actually the Italian translation sucked so much (it "translated" that line with "if an Eva doesn't have a soul I can merge with him", it has almost been directly copied from the anime, probably they want to give to the manga the same "feeling" of the anime, or maybe they wanted to transliterate that obscure line in a more clear way, but they did it really badly). I can only mention that "M" of the evangelion maling list translated that line as: "If I did not have a soul, we could join." the two translations basically match but surely that line is quite mysterious. It could make sense if he said that he has to remove his A.T. field and maybe this is the meaning of those lines but I'm really not sure about it.

[]: Re: Mistranslation in volume 11?             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Sep 07, 2010 10:45 am COACH wrote: The latter makes more sense to me, because I don't think Kaworu could do a whole lot of anything if he didn't have a soul. The Japanese line, 「魂さえなければ、同化できるさ。」 is pretty ambiguous, too. Literally, it's basically, "If only there was no soul, we could merge." Not too sure which translation is correct...

Article: Mistranslation in volume 11
[]: Mistranslation in volume 11?             Quote Post by COACH » Tue Sep 07, 2010 2:06 am So I finally got around to buying VIZ's translation of volume 11 of Sadamoto's manga, and I noticed an odd line: Kaworu, page 60 wrote: If I did not have a soul, we could be come one.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Sep 07, 2010 3:19 am Thank you for this thread, I didn't notice that actually the Italian translation sucked so much (it "translated" that line with "if an Eva doesn't have a soul I can merge with him", it has almost been directly copied from the anime, probably they want to give to the manga the same "feeling" of the anime, or maybe they wanted to transliterate that obscure line in a more clear way, but they did it really badly). I can only mention that "M" of the evangelion maling list translated that line as: "If I did not have a soul, we could join." the two translations basically match but surely that line is quite mysterious. It could make sense if he said that he has to remove his A.T. field and maybe this is the meaning of those lines but I'm really not sure about it.

[]: Re: Mistranslation in volume 11?             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Sep 07, 2010 10:45 am COACH wrote: The latter makes more sense to me, because I don't think Kaworu could do a whole lot of anything if he didn't have a soul. The Japanese line, 「魂さえなければ、同化できるさ。」 is pretty ambiguous, too. Literally, it's basically, "If only there was no soul, we could merge." Not too sure which translation is correct...
